stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates ongoing community interest and benchmarking activity around Meta's latest LLM releases.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, are receiving a global release.
The release follows US government action that previously placed curbs on the models.
The news signifies a major step in the commercial availability and deployment of frontier AI.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors formats.
It is associated with deepseek_v3 and conversational AI capabilities, indicating relevance in the LLM space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML frameworks like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 performance is significantly boosted in Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The speed improvement is achieved through Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Coding agent performance shows up to a 90% speed increase according to the Aider benchmark.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance boost results in faster response times and higher quality model outputs.
The updated engine also demonstrates improved memory efficiency when running models.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes local-first operation, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets and accelerates execution on Apple Silicon hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview release, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring data and processing remain on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding models (e.g., Claude Code, Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Xbox testing disc-to-digital feature that digitizes a physical game collection
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning to phase out physical disc production for Xbox games, mirroring Sony's move.
Xbox is developing a 'disc-to-digital' feature to allow current owners to digitize their physical game libraries.
This initiative suggests a transition toward a fully digital ecosystem while accommodating existing hardware investments.

stories.title: Sony is killing discs — and showing us why it’s a terrible idea
stories.author: Andrew Webster
bullet_points: Sony is discontinuing the production of physical PlayStation discs starting in January 2028.
Future PS5 game purchases will be exclusively digital after the announced cutoff date.
The move is framed by the article as a significant threat to video game preservation.

stories.title: US home battery installations hit record high on rising electricity costs
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Record installations of home battery storage are driven by increasing electricity costs.
This surge in residential storage capacity offers new capabilities for grid stabilization and management.
The growing distributed energy resources (DERs) are becoming increasingly relevant for supporting large consumers like AI data centers.

stories.title: Sony announces end of PlayStation discs, parts of digital store in the same day
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Sony is discontinuing the availability of PlayStation physical discs.
The change also affects certain parts of the PlayStation digital store.
The announcement signals a significant industry shift towards digital distribution for the platform.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical weakness in Large Language Models (LLMs): their inability to accurately express uncertainty.
The study introduces Reinforcement Learning combined with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring.
This work aims to reduce issues like high-confidence hallucination and poor knowledge boundary recognition, enhancing LLM trustworthiness.

stories.title: AxDafny: Agentic Verified Code Generation in Dafny
stories.author: Leopoldo Sarra
bullet_points: Introduces AxDafny, a framework for agentic code generation in Dafny that simultaneously produces executable code and formal proof artifacts.
The system uses a verifier-guided repair mechanism to iteratively generate code, invariants, assertions, and termination arguments.
Presents LiveCodeBench-Pro-Dafny (LCB-Pro-Dafny), a new benchmark suite of 250 competition-style problems for formal verification evaluation.

stories.title: Surrogate Fidelity: When Can Open LLMs Explain Closed Ones?
stories.author: Nehal Bandi
bullet_points: The paper addresses the 'surrogate problem' in Mechanistic Interpretability (MI), where closed LLMs lack full internal access.
It investigates when measurements from open LLMs can reliably infer behavior in proprietary, closed models.
Fidelity is evaluated across prediction, attribution, and representation levels, using log-odds as an API-compatible metric.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical LLM weakness: the inability to accurately express uncertainty and avoid confident hallucinations.
It proposes using Reinforcement Learning augmented with Metacognitive Feedback to improve LLM self-monitoring capabilities.
This work aims to significantly boost the trustworthiness and reliability of large language models by enhancing their internal awareness.

stories.title: Meta Caps Internal AI Token Spending After Costs Approach Billions in 2026
stories.author: typeofhuman
bullet_points: Meta is implementing spending caps on its internal AI token usage.
This action is being taken due to projected AI costs potentially reaching billions in 2026.
The news highlights the increasing operational expense and resource management challenges in large-scale AI development.

stories.title: US Supreme Court just blew up EU-US Data Transfers
stories.author: noyb.eu via kngl
bullet_points: The US Supreme Court has issued a ruling impacting EU-US data transfer mechanisms.
This decision potentially disrupts established data flow frameworks between the European Union and the United States.
The news signifies a major legal and regulatory shift affecting international data governance and compliance.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden the range of supported models and hardware.
It enhances Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
Designed specifically for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
Optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of 'subagents' for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added to enhance Claude Code's knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Xbox’s ‘reset’: all the news about Microsoft’s looming layoffs and studio closures
stories.author: Verge Staff
bullet_points: Xbox is undergoing a significant internal restructuring referred to as an 'Xbox reset'.
The changes were announced by CEO Asha Sharma and Chief Content Officer Matt Booty due to business challenges.
The memo cited issues such as a '3 percent accountability margin' as reasons for the overhaul.

stories.title: Comcast’s split could make or break Peacock
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Comcast is planning to split NBCUniversal, Peacock, and Sky from its broadband and wireless operations.
This corporate restructuring will force Peacock to operate independently.
The viability of Peacock's success in the streaming market is now uncertain without the backing of the larger, combined entity.

stories.title: T-Mobile moving tens of thousands of virtual machines off VMware amid lawsuit
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: T-Mobile is migrating a large volume of virtual machines away from VMware.
The move is occurring in the context of a lawsuit involving Broadcom.
T-Mobile is seeking continued support for its existing VMware perpetual licenses from Broadcom.

stories.title: NASA chief praises progress Blue Origin is making after launch failure
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA leadership acknowledges progress made by Blue Origin despite recent launch setbacks.
The statement suggests a relatively long timeline before significant concerns regarding Blue Origin's development are anticipated (into 2027).
The news highlights the ongoing competitive and developmental relationship between major aerospace players like NASA and Blue Origin.

stories.title: NASA inspector general suggests Boeing's Starliner will now be a decade late
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Inspector General has indicated a significant delay for Boeing's Starliner spacecraft.
The certification timeline for Starliner may now stretch to 2027.
This represents a substantial setback, pushing the schedule back a decade from Boeing's initial projections.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates how to train Language Models (LMs) to produce faithful self-explanations of their predictions.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a novel form of supervision for explanation training.
Findings suggest that LMs can track behavioral change even when trained on fixed explanations derived from past or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals for intermediate actions.
Highlights the common pitfall of evaluating dense supervision methods solely based on downstream performance.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit Data Referencing Errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when they understand the table structure.
DREs undermine the reliability of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This research provides the first systematic evaluation of DREs across various scenarios, moving beyond small-scale studies.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robot policy improvement, especially for long-horizon tasks.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to learn robot policies directly from human preferences.
FPL moves beyond binary comparisons by allowing annotators to define preferences in a more natural, freeform manner.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the critical issue of planning failure due to test-time distribution shift in traditional latent world models.
Implements test-time adaptation directly within the closed-loop framework of Model Predictive Control (MPC).

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (GRPO) where a single final outcome signal is used for credit assignment in agentic systems.
TRIAGE proposes a novel 'role-typed credit assignment framework' to better evaluate environment-facing actions (searches, clicks, navigation, etc.).
This framework aims to solve issues where standard methods unfairly penalize useful exploration or reinforce redundant actions.

stories.title: PolicyGuard: From Organizational Policies to Neuro-SymbolicCompliance Review Engines
stories.author: Amar Prakash Azad
bullet_points: PolicyGuard introduces a neuro-symbolic framework to address limitations in LLM-based policy compliance review.
It solves the issue where end-to-end LLM prompting leaves applied policy logic implicit, hindering inspection and testing.
The system converts organizational policies into a structured format for robust, verifiable compliance checking.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates whether training Language Models (LMs) to generate explanations leads to genuine 'faithful introspection' or just superficial imitation.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a novel form of supervision for explanation generation.
Surprisingly, the models can achieve behavioral change even when trained on fixed counterfactual explanations derived from older or similar models.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
Proposes QVal, a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals used to guide intermediate actions.
Highlights the current limitation in evaluating dense supervision methods by relying solely on downstream performance.

stories.title: Freeform Preference Learning for Robotic Manipulation
stories.author: Chelsea Finn
bullet_points: Addresses the bottleneck of reward design in autonomous robotic manipulation.
Introduces Freeform Preference Learning (FPL) to learn policies from human preferences.
Allows annotators to provide nuanced feedback beyond simple binary comparisons of trajectories.

stories.title: AdaJEPA: An Adaptive Latent World Model
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces AdaJEPA, an adaptive latent world model designed to improve planning capabilities.
Addresses the limitation of traditional latent world models by enabling test-time adaptation within Model Predictive Control (MPC).
Aims to prevent planning failures caused by inaccurate predictions or test-time distribution shift.

stories.title: Automated Background Swapping for Robustness against Spurious Backgrounds
stories.author: Kajetan Schweighofer
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of spurious correlations in Deep Neural Networks, where models rely on non-causal features.
Focuses specifically on the vision domain, identifying background elements as a common source of these misleading correlations.
Proposes an 'Automated Background Swapping' technique to enhance model robustness against such spurious background dependencies.

stories.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Alborz Geramifard
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in standard Reinforcement Learning (RL) where final outcomes are used uniformly for credit assignment.
Introduces TRIAGE, a novel role-typed credit assignment framework for Agentic RL.
Aims to improve RL by preventing the punishment of useful exploration and reducing reinforcement of redundant actions.

stories.title: FedLAB: Traceable Semantic Codebooks for Federated Multimodal Graph Foundation Learning
stories.author: Guoren Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of training multimodal graph foundation models on decentralized, privacy-constrained data.
It introduces 'FedLAB,' a method utilizing Traceable Semantic Codebooks to enable federated learning for these complex models.
The goal is to allow models to learn reusable knowledge from graphs containing diverse data types (text, images, attributes) without centralizing raw data.

stories.title: CoMet: Context and Multiplicity Decomposition for Multimodal Uncertainty Estimation
stories.author: Olga Russakovsky
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'CoMet,' a method for estimating uncertainty in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Uncertainty estimation is highlighted as a critical, long-standing challenge in AI, analogous to metacognition.
CoMet addresses the complexity of uncertainty arising from diverse sources and their relationships within multimodal systems.

stories.title: Introspective Coupling: Self-Explanation Training Tracks Behavioral Change Despite Fixed Supervision
stories.author: Belinda Z. Li
bullet_points: The research investigates how to train Language Models (LMs) to produce faithful self-explanations of their predictions.
The study uses counterfactual behavior on modified inputs as a form of supervision for the explanation training.
Findings suggest that LMs can track behavioral change even when trained on fixed, historical, or similar model explanations.

stories.title: QVal: Cheaply Evaluating Dense Supervision Signals for Long-Horizon LLM Agents
stories.author: Matthias Bethge
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of sparse rewards in long-horizon LLM agent tasks.
It focuses on 'QVal,' a method for cheaply evaluating dense supervision signals used to guide intermediate actions.
The research critiques the common practice of evaluating dense supervision methods solely based on downstream performance.

stories.title: When LLMs Read Tables Carelessly: Measuring and Reducing Data Referencing Errors
stories.author: Huzefa Rangwala
bullet_points: LLMs exhibit Data Referencing Errors (DREs) when processing tables, even when they grasp the table structure.
DREs are critical because they undermine the correctness of intermediate reasoning steps, not just the final answer.
This research provides the first systematic, large-scale evaluation of these tabular data referencing errors.

stories.title: Generative Skill Composition for LLM Agents
stories.author: Tianlong Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of selecting appropriate 'skill compositions' for LLM agents, which use modular knowledge packages to solve complex tasks.
Skills allow LLM agents to perform specialized functions like environment setup, running tests, or code refactoring.
The core bottleneck identified is how to effectively compose and select from growing, reusable skill libraries.

stories.title: Scalable Behaviour Cloning on Browser Using via Skill Distillation
stories.author: Qinhuai Na
bullet_points: Proposes using human web browsing behavior as a scalable source of reusable skills for AI agents.
Identifies decision-making under incomplete information as the primary bottleneck for browser agents, not low-level operations.
Introduces a method, 'Skill Distillation,' to leverage implicit knowledge present in human interaction traces.

stories.title: DigitalCoach: Communication and Grounding Gaps in Human and Agentic Computer Use Coaching
stories.author: Amy Pavel
bullet_points: Introduces 'DigitalCoach,' a multimodal dataset capturing human expert-novice computer use coaching sessions.
The dataset includes 22,752 dialogue turns grounded in screen and input event recordings across five software applications.
The research aims to evaluate the capability of state-of-the-art AI models to effectively teach humans software usage.

stories.title: MECoBench: A Systematic Study of Multimodal Agent Collaboration in Embodied Environments
stories.author: Zhongyu Wei
bullet_points: Introduces MECoBench, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal agent collaboration in embodied environments.
Addresses the gap in current research regarding how MLLMs collaborate in visually grounded, real-world tasks.
Evaluates various MLLMs across diverse tasks, cooperation structures, and collaboration modes.

stories.title: For first time, a cell built from scratch grows and divides
stories.author: defrost
bullet_points: Researchers have successfully created a cell from scratch that is capable of growth and division.
This represents a significant step in synthetic biology and cellular engineering.
The research is published/detailed on the Biotic.org platform.

stories.title: Opening up 'Zero-Knowledge Proof' technology to promote privacy in age assurance
stories.author: consumer451
bullet_points: Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) technology is being explored for age assurance.
The primary goal of this application is to enhance user privacy.
The discussion originates from Hacker News, suggesting a technical/developer audience interest.

stories.title: Reading Anthropic's "When AI Builds Itself" Changed How I Think About AI and Software Engineering
stories.author: hemapriya_kanagala
bullet_points: The article discusses Anthropic's essay, "When AI Builds Itself."
The essay explores the concept of AI self-improvement and autonomous development.
It is relevant to both AI research and software engineering practices.
